data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build binary log (binlog) file to diagnose build failures. Let me start by examining the binlog file and extracting error information from it.

Binary log files (.binlog) are MSBuild binary logs. I'll need to use the `dotnet` CLI or a tool to read them. Let me check what tools are available and try to extract the errors.

arguments.command:
# Install structured log viewer CLI tool to parse binlog
dotnet tool install -g dotnet-structuredlogviewer 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool install -g MSBuild.StructuredLogger 2>/dev/null || true
# Try using MSBuild replay to extract errors/warnings
dotnet msbuild build.binlog -flp:logfile=/tmp/build.log -nologo 2>&1 | tail -50
arguments.description: Try replaying binlog to extract errors

data.content:
Here's the diagnosis:

---

## Build Failure Analysis — `BuildDiag.sln`

### Solution structure (dependency graph)
```
Core.csproj          ← builds successfully ✅
Services.csproj      ← references Core — FAILS ❌ (root cause)
Api.csproj           ← references Services — FAILS ❌ (cascading)
```

### Root Cause — `Services/Services.csproj`

**3 errors, all from `Services/UserService.cs`**, all caused by a single issue:

| Error | Location | Message |
|-------|----------|---------|
| CS0246 | Line 2, col 7 | `Newtonsoft` namespace not found |
| CS0246 | Line 3, col 7 | `Newtonsoft` namespace not found |
| CS0246 | Line 22, col 19 | `JObject` type not found |

**Diagnosis:** `UserService.cs` uses `using Newtonsoft.Json;` and `using Newtonsoft.Json.Linq;`, but **`Services.csproj` does not declare a `PackageReference` to `Newtonsoft.Json`**. The Csc compiler received only framework assemblies and `Core.dll` as references — no Newtonsoft assembly was present.

### Cascading Failure — `Api/Api.csproj`

Api has a `ProjectReference` to Services. Since Services failed to compile, `ResolveProjectReferences` failed in Api, and it never reached its own compile step. **Api has no errors of its own.**

### Fix

Add the missing NuGet package reference to `Services/Services.csproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This single fix will resolve all 3 errors and unblock the Api project as well.
